text.title: procurement-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for procurement-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Lowest price wins without quality and risk weighting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Price-only selection consistently underestimates ramp-up cost, quality gap, and vendor management overhead; total cost of engagement typically exceeds a higher-priced reliable vendor.

failure-mode.description: Reference checks summarized from the vendor's own proposal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Self-reported references are marketing; independent reference checks require contacting previous clients directly, not reading testimonials in the bid document.

failure-mode.description: Vague SOW accepted to start faster.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every undefined deliverable becomes a dispute; a clear SOW is the single most important document in procurement — spending one week on it saves months of rework.

failure-mode.description: No change process defined in the contract.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scope always evolves; without a written change process, every addition is either rejected or silently absorbed until someone notices the cost overrun.
